May 17, 1985 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our No. A-85-117 
We are in receipt of your letter of May 13, 1985, seeking 
advice regarding Edward K. Paul, member of the Planning 
Commission of the City of Oxnard. 
Unfortunately, we cannot 
provide you with either an Opinion (Gov. Code Section 83114(a») 
or with formal written advice (Gov. Code Section 83114(b) , 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329 (newly adopted» 
at this time. 
I 
enclose a copy of the just-adopted regulation for your review. 
Before specific written advice could be provided we would need 
Mr. Paul's version of the facts, we do not provide advice based 
upon facts provided by third parties. 
If Mr. Paul wishes us to 
provide him with formal written advice, he should provide us 
with all the material facts. 
He may do this directly or he may 
have you act as his authorized representative. 
At this time, I will treat your letter of May 13 as a 
request for informal assistance. 
I enclose a copy of the 
Commission's Nord Opinion, 8 FPPC Opinions 6, No. 83-004; and 
our regulations dealing with otherwise related business 
entities, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18236 and 18706. 
As you can 
see, these interpretations of the law are rather narrow in scope 
and apply only where substantial ownership or operational 
connections exist between the two business entities in question. 


K. D. Lyders 
May 17, 1985 
Page 2 
If Mr. Paul does desire further assistance, please do not 
hesitate to contact this office.

In response to your request for written advice from the 
Commission, I have reviewed the advice letter from City Attorney 
K.D. Lyders regarding your situation and I concur in his 
conclusions, although not in every aspect of his analysis. 
However, I believe the conclusion is what you desire.

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
RE: 
YOUR No. A-85-117 
DEAR MR. LEIDIGH, 
WITH REFERENCE TO YOUR COMMUNICATIONS WITH OXNARD 
CITY ATTORNEY, K.O. LYDERS, I DO REQUEST FORMAL 
WRITTEN ADVICE. 
THE OXNARD BUSINESS RESEARCH PARK IS A RATHER 
LARGE AND LONG-TERM DEVELOPMENT PROPOSED FOR 
OXNARD. 
ONE OF THE PRINCIPALS IN THAT PROPOSED 
DEVELOPMENT IS MR. 
MARTIN V. SMITH. 
IT IS CORRECT THAT MR. SMITH IS A LANDLORD FOR 
CARRIAGE SQUARE WINE & SPIRITS, A BUSINESS VENTURE 
OXNARD. CA 93031 
OF MINE. 
WHEN I PURCHASED THE STORE IN NOVEMBER, 1980, 
I ASSUMED THE OBLIGATIONS OF AN EXISTING LEASE THAT 
RUNS TO JUNE 1992. 
IN 1983, I EXPANDED THE STORE 
AND ENTERED INTO A NEW LEASE WITH THE SAME EXPIRATION 
DATE AND SAME PERCENTAGE CLAUSE AS THE OLD LEASE BUT 
WITH A CURRENT MARKET RATE FOR BASE RENT. 
IT IS MY 
UNDERSTANDING THAT THESE WERE THE SAME TERMS BEING 
OFFERED TO OTHER TENANTS AT THE TIME. 
IT IS ALSO CORRECT THAT I 
AM ONE OF SEVEN ORGANIZERS 
FOR CHANNEL ISLANDS NATIONAL BANK (PROPOSED). 
Two OF 
THE OTHER ORGANIZERS ARE MENTIONED, MR. 
GARY O'BRIEN, 
A VICE-PRESIDENT OF THE MARTIN V. SMITH ORGANIZATION 
AND MR. STANLEY COHEN, LEGAL COUNSEL FOR MR. SMITH. 


My INTEREST IN THE PROPOSED BANK AND RELATIONSHIP 
WITH MESSRS. SMITH, O'BRIEN OR COHEN APPEAR TO BE 
THE QUESTION. 
ASIDE FROM THE STORE LEASE, I 
HAVE NO ARRANGEMENTS 
WITH MR. SMITH. 
THE PROPOSED BANK IS AN EXISTING 
BRANCH OF UNION BANK AND SEVERAL MILES FROM THE 
PROPOSED DEVELOPMENT. 
I WOULD BE QUITE WILLING TO SUPPLY ANY OTHER 
INFORMATION YOU MAY REQUEST. 
VERY TRULY YOURS, 
EDWARD F. PAUL 
PLANNING COMMISSIONER 
CITY OF OXNARD 
CC: 
K. D. L YDERS 
EFP:GSS 


CITY OF 
~~narO 
CITY ATTORNEY • 300 W. THIRD STREET • OXNARD, CALIFORNIA 93030 • (805) 486-4311 EXT. 2270 
1<' DUA1\iE lYDERS, CITY ATTORI'.EY 
Edward F. Paul 
1964 Rhonda 
Oxnard, Ca 
93030 
May 21, 1985 
Re: 
Oxnard Business-Research Park: 
Alleged Conflict of Interest 
Dear Mr. Paul: 
Enclosed is information received from the Fair Political Practices 
Commission regarding my request for advice dated May 1J, 1985. 
The 
Commission did not answer my request directly but enclosed the Nord 
opinion and other information dealing with otherwise related business 
entities. 
Based upon the information received, it does not appear to 
me that a conflict of interest exists which would require your 
disqualification in matters relating to the Oxnard Business-Research 
Park. 
The letter dated May 8, 1985, from Mrs. Maurer and Mrs. Johnson raised 
two areas of possible conflict. 
The first involves your relationship 
to Martin V. Smith as a tenant in the Carriage Square Shopping Center. 
The first question is whether your leasehold interest has a bonus 
value over $1,000.00, thereby constituting an investment under section 
87103 of the Government Code. 
Assuming that it did, then you would be 
disqualified from any decision having a material financial affect on 
Martin V. Smith or any "related business entity". 
The test for a related 
business entity is whether substantial ownership or operational connections 
exists between the two business entities in question. 
If it were shown 
that Martin V. Smith had the controlling interest in the Oxnard Business-
Research Park, then the conflict situation might exist. 
On the other hand, 
if your leasehold interest was worth less than $1,000.00 or if Martin V. 
Smith does not have the controlling interest in Oxnard Business-Research 
Park, then no such conflict would exist. 


Edward F. Paul 
t1ay 21, 1985 
Page 2 
The second basis for an alleged conflict was the fact that you are a 
director and ~hareholder of the proposed Channel Islands National Bank. 
It does not appear that Martin V. Smith has any interest in this bank 
at the present time. 
However, his attorney, Mr. Cohen, and a Vice-
President of companies owned by Martin V. Smith are also directors and 
shareholders of the proposed bank. 
None of the directors will have more 
than five percent ownership of the bank's stock. 
There does not appear 
to be a financial or ownership connection between the bank and the 
Oxnard Business-Research Park. 
Under these facts, it is my opinion that 
there is no conflict which would require your disqualification in decisions 
affecting the Oxnard Business-Research Park. 
If any of my facts or assumptions are incorrect, I would appreciate your 
letting me know. 
If you desire additional assistance from the Fair 
Political Practices Commission, please either contact them directly or 
authorize me in writing to seek their advice.

Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
May 13, 1985 
Re: 
Opinion Request: 
Edward K. Paul, Oxnard Planning Commissioner 
Gentlemen: 
1 
An opinion is requested with respect to the information contained in the 
attached letter dated May 8, 1985, concerning Edward K. Paul, member of 
Planning Commission of the City of Oxnard. 
Your advice is sought because 
of the recently enacted regulations dealing with otherwise related 
business entities with which we are generally unfamiliar at the local 
level. 
As indicated in the attached documents, Mr. Paul, as member of the Planning 
Commission, would be making decisions with regard to a proposed development 
known as the Oxnard Business-Research Park. 
Principals in the development 
of the Oxnard Business-Research Park are also principals in the establishment 
of the Channel Islands National Bank. 
So far as I am aware, however, there 
is no financial or other relationship between the business park and the bank 
and geographically the two would be separated by approximately five miles. 
If you need any additional information to respond to this request please let 
me know. 
Your prompt response would be very much appreciated. 
fd 
Enclosures 
cc: 
Ed Paul 
Dick r,1aggio 
r,1rs. John t·1aurer 
Mrs. Cherie Johnson 


Mrs Joan Maurer 
Mrs Cherie Johnson 
Sea Air Neighborhood Council 
2611 Taffrail Lane 
Seaview Neighborhood Council 
3451 Oarfish Lane 
Oxnard, CA 
93030 
Phone #985-3560 
Mr. K. Duane Lyders 
City Attorney of Oxnard 
305 West 3RD Street 
Oxnard, CA 
93030 
Dear Mr. Lyders: 
Oxnard, CA 
93030 
Phone #985-2927 
May 8, 1985 
o 
We, as representatives of the Sea Air and Seaview Neighborhood Cgancils, 
are opposed to the proposed development of the Oxnard Business/Research 
Park that is planned on the 185 open space acreage south of 5th Street 
and east of Victoria Boulevard. 
Information recently obtained by us indicates the possibility that 
Mr. Edward K. Paul, one of the Planning Commissioners who will be a 
part of the decision making process concerning the Oxnard Business/ 
Research Park, may have a conflict of interest in this matter. 
Mr. Paul is participating as an organizer and director of the new Channel 
Islands National Bank in partnership with Mr. Stanley Cohen and Mr. Gary 
L. O'Brien, both of whom are representatives or officers in the Martin V. 
Smith organization, one of the major developers of the proposed park. 
This business relationship is a matter of public record and is documented, 
please see attachments. 
Also, Mr. Paul is directly associated with 
Mr. Martin V. Smith in a landlord/tennant business relationship as 
owner and proprietor of Carriage Square Liquor Store. 
Based on his current business associations with the Martin V. Smith 
organization, we find Mr. Edward K. Paul in a potentially compromising 
position regarding this important issue. 
We feel that an unbiased, 
impartial evaluation and vote on the issue of the Oxnard Business/ 
Research Park would be virtually impossible for him to make under 
existing circumstances. 
We, on behalf of our respective Neighborhood 
Councils. respectfully ask that you investigate the potential conflict 
of interest. 
If your office determines that there is indeed a conflict 
of interest, we would hope that Mr. Paul would disqualify himself by 
refraining from all discussion of the matter in open Commission meetings, 
and abstain from voting on the Business/Research P&rk )roject or zoing 
changes related to it. 
Should Mr. Paul elect not to "step down" 
on the Business/Research Park 
issue, we will ask him in public forum to justify his position for the 
record and will also cogently pursue this matter through our City 
Council to our satisfaction. 
The City Planning Commission plans to 
address the Business/Research Park issue in its June 6th meeting, we 
hope to resolve this matter before then. 
Respectfully awaiting your earliest reply to this urgent issue. 
write or call. 
Please

Your letter, received on May 24, 1985, requesting 
advice under the'Political Reform Act has been referred to 
Robert Leidigh, an attorney in the Legal Division of the 
Fair Political Practices Commission. 
If you have any 
questions about your advice request, you may contact this 
attorney directly· at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or u~less more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days.

IT IS MY 
UNDERSTANDING THAT THESE WERE THE SAME TERMS BEING 
OFFERED TO OTHER TENANTS AT THE TIME. 
IT IS ALSO CORRECT THAT I 
AM ONE OF SEVEN ORGANIZERS 
FOR CHANNEL ISLANDS NATIONAL BANK (PROPOSED). 
Two OF 
THE OTHER ORGANIZERS ARE MENTIONED, MR. 
GARY O'BRIEN, 
A VICE-PRESIDENT OF THE MARTIN V. SMITH ORGANIZATION 
AND MR. STANLEY COHEN, LEGAL COUNSEL FOR MR. SMITH. 


My INTEREST IN THE PROPOSED BANK AND RELATIONSHIP 
WITH MESSRS. SMITH, O'BRIEN OR COHEN APPEAR TO BE 
THE QUESTION. 
ASIDE FROM THE STORE LEASE, I 
HAVE NO ARRANGEMENTS 
WITH MR. SMITH. 
THE PROPOSED BANK IS AN EXISTING 
BRANCH OF UNION BANK AND SEVERAL MILES FROM THE 
PROPOSED DEVELOPMENT. 
I WOULD BE QUITE WILLING TO SUPPLY ANY OTHER 
INFORMATION YOU MAY REQUEST.

Lyders City Attorney City of Oxnard 300 W. Third Street Oxnard, CA 93030 Dear Mr. Lyders: Technical Assistance •• Administration • If Executive/legal 322·5901 Enforcement (916) 322·5662 322·5660 322-6441 May 17, 1985 Re: Your Request for Advice Our No. A-85-117 We are in receipt of your letter of May 13, 1985, seeking advice regarding Edward K. Paul, member of the Planning Commission of the City of Oxnard. Unfortunately, we cannot provide you with either an Opinion (Gov. Code Section 83114(a») or with formal written advice (Gov. Code Section 83114(b) , 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329 (newly adopted» at this time. I enclose a copy of the just-adopted regulation for your review. Before specific written advice could be provided we would need Mr. Paul's version of the facts, we do not provide advice based upon facts provided by third parties. If Mr. Paul wishes us to provide him with formal written advice, he should provide us with all the material facts. He may do this directly or he may have you act as his authorized representative. At this time, I will treat your letter of May 13 as a request for informal assistance. I enclose a copy of the Commission's Nord Opinion, 8 FPPC Opinions 6, No. 83-004; and our regulations dealing with otherwise related business entities, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18236 and 18706. As you can see, these interpretations of the law are rather narrow in scope and apply only where substantial ownership or operational connections exist between the two business entities in question. K. D. Lyders May 17, 1985 Page 2 If Mr. Paul does desire further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact this office. REL:plh Enclosures cc: Edward K. Paul Joan M.
